Caption: Cavitation dynamics of two bubbles with (w) or without (w/o) considering the interactions between bubbles. (a)–(c) Relative radius ( R 1 ′ = R 1 t / R 10 ), internal temperature ( T 1 ) and pressure ( P 1 ) of the small bubble as a function of time, while panels (d)–(f) denote the corresponding results for the large bubble, respectively.

Time evolution of the chemical yields ( n k , i ) within the (a)–(b) small and (c)–(d) large bubbles. In the legends, the ‘w’ and ‘w/o’ denote the results with (w) or without (w/o) considering the interactions between bubbles, respectively.

Maximum values of the (a) bubble expansion ratio R max ′ , (b) temperature T max , and (c) pressure P max , as well as (d) the total chemical yields n tot within the small bubble as a function of the ultrasound amplitude (PA = 1.2–2 atm).
Variations of (a) R max ′ , (b) T max , (c) P max and (d) n tot at various ultrasound frequencies (f = 20–200 kHz).

Effects of the distance between bubbles (d 0 = 80–300 µm) and the ambient radius of the large bubble (R 20 = 2–10 µm) on the acoustic cavitation characteristics of a 2-µm-radius bubble. (a–d) The differences of R max ′ , T max , P max and n tot between two cases with (w) and without (w/o) considering the interaction effects ( Δ X = X w - X w / o , where X denotes the observed variable, i.e., R max ′ , T max , P max and n tot ).

Influences of inter-bubble interactions on the acoustic cavitation characteristics, (i.e., (a) Δ R max ′ , (b) Δ T max , (c) Δ P max and (d) Δ n tot ) with respect to the surface tension (σ = 10–100 mN/m) and the viscosity (μ = 1–10 mPa·s) of the surrounding liquid.
